prompt:
请总结下面长文档的核心观点，并列出三个可执行的工程建议：

TensorRT-LLM profiling should separate prefill and decode. Prefill reads the full prompt, builds attention state, and fills KV cache. Decode generates one token at a time and repeatedly reads model weights plus cached keys and values. A short chat workload can hide prefill cost, while a long document summary workload can make TTFT dominate the user experience.

A production inference report should record model id, checkpoint revision, engine path, TensorRT-LLM version, GPU model, driver version, maximum sequence length, maximum batch size, KV cache fraction, benchmark dataset, concurrency, warmup count, request count, sampling parameters, and raw JSON outputs. Without these fields, two benchmark numbers are rarely comparable.

请从下面材料里提炼出 profiling 闭环：

When concurrency increases, LLM serving systems can batch more decode steps together. This often raises total output token throughput, but it may also increase request latency and KV cache pressure. The useful operating point is usually before throughput flattens and before p95 or p99 latency becomes unacceptable.

Quantization reduces weight precision and can reduce memory bandwidth pressure. It may improve tokens per second and reduce engine size, but it can also change output quality. A quantized model must be compared against the same text workload and, ideally, a small quality checklist in addition to raw speed metrics.

阅读下面内容，说明哪些指标属于 workload shape，哪些指标属于 performance result：

Input length, output length, request count, warmup count, concurrency, TTFT, TPOT, request throughput, output token throughput, p95 latency, GPU utilization, memory used, engine size, dtype, quantization, tensor parallel size, pipeline parallel size.

请基于下面背景给出一个单卡推理调参顺序：

The engine uses bfloat16, max sequence length 4096, maximum batch size 8, paged KV cache, remove input padding, and context FMHA. The GPU has 8GB memory. The first smoke run shows throughput increasing from concurrency 1 to 4, but flattening at 8.
